title: Beyond Functional Correctness: Code Quality and HumanAI Interaction in a Quasi-Experimental Comparison of AI-Assisted Non-Programmers and Programmers
data:
LM Esnaola, HD Ramn, LC Lanzarini - 2026
Purpose: We extend a nonequivalent-groups quasi-experiment comparing 
AIassisted non-programmers (NP-AI) and programmers without AI (P-noAI) on three 
Python challenges (CB/CI/CA) by analyzing internal code quality beyond test 
passing, associations between humanAI interaction quality and outcomes within NP-
AI, and basic structural code metrics. Methods: Twenty-six NP-AI and twenty-six P-
noAI participants each solved one randomly assigned challenge. Submitted

title: Aligning Code Language Models With Human Cognitive Patterns
data:
Y Zhang - 2026
The rapid integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into software engineering 
has exposed a fundamental disconnect between the probabilistic nature of machine 
code generation and the structured, intent-driven cognitive processes of human 
developers. This dissertation addresses this gap by proposing a unified framework 
for Cognitive Alignment, a system designed to ground model behavior in observable 
human utility and comprehension rather than mere statistical likelihood. By treating
